rag_rules: Fix unresolved references and type mismatches in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible and types match.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions to avoid '<error>' type issues.
Place AGREE contracts in valid scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for assignments.
Validate that all AGREE declarations (const, eq) use correct scalar types: int, real, bool.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the same scope.
Match expression types in binary operations: bool, int, or real must be consistent on both sides.
Avoid introducing package-qualified names or types not present in the AADL model.
Use only named constants or visible features in component type contracts for assumptions and guarantees.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared variables.
Binary expressions in AGREE require matching operand types.
Constants and equations in AGREE must declare valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers or types that are not declared or visible in the current AADL scope.
Do not mix incompatible types in binary expressions (e.g., bool and int).
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use only assignments.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the model dependencies.
irrelevant_topics: Component type vs implementation scope distinctions
